name: Habshi ibn Qays
title: Brave of Nahm
shortDescription: One of the braves of the Nahm tribe and sincere Shias of Kufa who reached Imam Hussain's (AS) army and was martyred.
fullStory:
Habshi (or Habshali) ibn Qays al-Nahmi was a brave man and a sincere Shia in Kufa. His tribe (Bani Nahm) was among the tribes that had a special devotion to Amir al-Mu'minin (AS).

When Habshi heard that Imam Hussain (AS) was surrounded by Ibn Ziyad's army in Karbala, despite all the strict security measures in Kufa, he managed to leave the city and reach the caravan of light.

On the day of Ashura, he stood bravely against the army of darkness and, after a valiant fight, attained martyrdom before the noon prayer (or in the first attack).

The name of this loyal companion is mentioned in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa and Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya, receiving greetings from the infallible Imam.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى حَبَشِيِّ بْنِ قَيْسٍ النَّهْمِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Habshi ibn Qays al-Nahmi...
